metadata.title: WORSHEEP
metadata.description: Sheet music management & setlist app for worship teams

footer.description: Sheet music management for worship teams

onboarding.step1Desc: Search for songs by title, artist, or lyrics.
onboarding.step1Tip: 💡 Turn on AI search to find songs with natural language like "upbeat praise songs"!
onboarding.step2Desc: Click songs to select them, then download as PDF/PPT or save as a setlist from the top bar.
onboarding.step2Tip: 💡 Use the "Song Form" button on song cards to set the song order (intro, verse 1, bridge, etc.).
onboarding.step3Desc: Tap the pencil icon on a song card to write notes directly on sheet music.
onboarding.step3Tip: 💡 Your annotated sheets are auto-saved in My Page > My Notes.
onboarding.step4Desc: When you sign up, you'll automatically join the WORSHEEP team. Open sample setlists and try out team features!
onboarding.step4Tip: 💡 Once you're familiar, create your own team. You can leave the demo team anytime.
onboarding.step5Desc: Let us know if something's not working right or if you have feature ideas!
onboarding.step5Tip: 💡 Tap the chat bubble button at the bottom-right to send feedback.

main.landingSubtitle: Upload the songs your team uses and prepare setlists in one go.
main.landingFeaturesTitle: Everything for worship prep, in one place
main.landingFeature1Desc: Upload and search PDF sheet music, and annotate right on the score.
main.landingFeature2Desc: Drag to arrange the service order and share it with your team.
main.landingFeature3Desc: Prepare together in a song library only your team can see.
main.landingFeature4Desc: Cast the service view to a big screen for the congregation.
main.onboardingSubtitle: Create a team or join with an invite code to open your song library.
main.onboardingCreateDesc: Create a new team and become its leader
main.onboardingJoinDesc: Join an existing team with an invite code
main.aiSearchPlaceholder: Search with natural language (e.g. fast praise songs for Easter)
main.loginRequiredSetlist: Please log in to save setlists.
main.loginRequiredNotebook: Please log in to save notebooks.

terms.article1Content: These Terms govern the conditions, procedures, rights and obligations between the Company and Users regarding the use of sheet music search, setlist management, team collaboration, and other services provided by WORSHEEP (hereinafter "the Service").
terms.article2Content:
1. "Service" refers to all related services provided by the Company, including sheet music viewing, setlist management, team management, and PDF/PPT generation.
2. "User" refers to both members and non-members who use the Service in accordance with these Terms.
3. "Member" refers to a person who has registered for the Service and been assigned a user account.
4. "Sheet Music" refers to music-related content such as scores, chords, and lyrics provided within the Service.
5. "Setlist" refers to a set of sheet music arranged in order for worship services or performances.
6. "Official Sheet Music" refers to sheet music provided through formal agreements with copyright holders or rights owners.
7. "User Sheet Music" refers to sheet music directly uploaded by Users.
terms.article3Content:
1. These Terms take effect when posted on the Service screen or otherwise notified to Users.
2. The Company may amend these Terms as necessary within the scope permitted by applicable laws.
3. When the Terms are amended, the Company shall announce the changes and effective date through in-service notices at least 7 days prior to the effective date.
4. If a User does not agree to the amended Terms, they may discontinue use of the Service and withdraw their membership.
terms.article4Intro: 1. The Company provides the following services:
terms.article4Item3: Team creation and team setlist management
terms.article4Item4: Sheet music annotation and memo features
terms.article4Item5: PDF and PPT file generation and download
terms.article4Item6: Other services determined by the Company
terms.article4Continued: 2. The Service is available 24 hours a day, 365 days a year in principle. However, it may be temporarily suspended for system maintenance or other necessary reasons.
terms.article5Content:
1. Users may apply for membership registration according to the procedures established by the Company.
2. The Company may refuse registration in the following cases:
terms.article5Item3: Other cases deemed inappropriate for registration
terms.article6Intro: Users shall not engage in the following activities:
terms.article6Item2: Unauthorized modification of information posted by the Company
terms.article6Item3: Collecting, storing, or disclosing information not authorized by the Company
terms.article6Item4: Infringing on intellectual property rights, including copyrights of the Company or third parties
terms.article6Item5: Damaging the reputation of the Company or third parties
terms.article6Item6: Commercially using information obtained through the Service without prior consent of the Company
terms.article6Item7: Other activities that violate applicable laws and regulations
terms.article7Sub1Content: All copyrights for elements constituting the Service, including design, text, scripts, graphics, and logos, belong to the Company.
terms.article7Sub2Content:
1. Official Sheet Music: Provided through formal agreements with copyright holders and may only be used for purposes within the Service.

2. User Sheet Music: Copyright responsibility for sheet music uploaded by Users lies with the respective User.

3. When uploading sheet music, Users confirm and agree to the following:
terms.article7Sub2Item1: They hold the copyright to the sheet music, or
terms.article7Sub2Item2: They have obtained lawful permission from the copyright holder, or
terms.article7Sub2Item3: The sheet music is in the public domain with an expired copyright protection period
terms.article7Sub2Continued: 4. The Company may immediately remove sheet music upon receiving a copyright infringement report, and may restrict the User's access to the Service in cases of repeated copyright infringement.
terms.article7Sub3Content:
1. Users are responsible for any copyright disputes arising from content they post within the Service.
2. If the Company faces legal action, including claims for damages from third parties due to a User's copyright infringement, the User shall indemnify the Company and compensate for all resulting damages.
terms.article7CopyrightNote: For detailed copyright policies, please refer to our <link>Copyright Policy</link>.
terms.article8Intro: The Company may restrict service use or revoke membership in the following cases:
terms.article8Item4: Other cases deemed inappropriate for service use
terms.article9Content:
1. The Company may modify or suspend all or part of the Service as needed for business policies and operations.
2. In case of service suspension, the Company shall provide notice through in-service announcements 30 days in advance. However, suspension without prior notice may occur in unavoidable circumstances.
terms.article10Content:
1. The Company is exempt from liability when unable to provide the Service due to force majeure events such as natural disasters, war, or suspension of telecommunications services.
2. The Company is not liable for service disruptions caused by the User's own fault.
3. The Company is not liable for any expected profits lost by Users through use of the Service.
4. The Company is not responsible for the reliability or accuracy of information, materials, or facts posted by Users.
5. The Company does not guarantee the copyright legality of sheet music uploaded by Users and is not liable for any disputes arising therefrom.
terms.article11Content:
1. The Company may offer certain services on a paid basis.
2. Usage fees, payment methods, and refund policies for paid services shall be posted separately within the applicable service.
3. When paying for paid services, Users must use payment methods under their own name and may not use another person's payment method without authorization.
terms.article12Content: The Company has established and complies with a Privacy Policy to protect Users' personal information. For details, please refer to our <link>Privacy Policy</link>.
terms.article13Content:
1. Lawsuits regarding disputes between the Company and Users shall be governed by the laws of the Republic of Korea.
2. In case of disputes between the Company and Users related to service use, both parties shall negotiate in good faith to resolve the matter.
terms.supplementContent: These Terms shall take effect from December 1, 2024.

privacy.section1Title: 1. Purpose of Collecting and Using Personal Information
privacy.section1Intro: WORSHEEP (hereinafter "the Service") collects and uses personal information for the following purposes:
privacy.section1Item1: Member registration and management: Identity verification and identification for membership services
privacy.section1Item2: Service provision: Worship song management, setlist creation, PDF/PPT generation features
privacy.section1Item3: Team collaboration: Support for collaboration features among team members
privacy.section1Item4: Service improvement: Analysis and improvement of service usage statistics
privacy.section2OptionalItem2: Profile image (when signing in with Google)
privacy.section3Title: 3. Retention and Use Period of Personal Information
privacy.section3Content:
1. Personal information is destroyed immediately upon membership withdrawal.
2. However, if retention is required by applicable laws, information shall be retained for the prescribed period:
  - Service usage records: 3 months (Protection of Communications Secrets Act)
privacy.section4Title: 4. Disclosure of Personal Information to Third Parties
privacy.section4Intro: The Service does not, in principle, provide Users' personal information to third parties. However, exceptions apply in the following cases:
privacy.section4Item1: When the User has given prior consent
privacy.section4Item2: When required by law or requested through legal procedures for investigative purposes
privacy.section5Title: 5. Measures to Ensure Security of Personal Information
privacy.section5Intro: The Service takes the following measures to ensure the security of personal information:
privacy.section5Item3: Utilization of Supabase security features (Row Level Security)
privacy.section6Intro: Users may exercise the following rights at any time:
privacy.section6Item2: Request to correct or delete personal information
privacy.section6Item3: Request to suspend processing of personal information
privacy.section6Note: These rights may be exercised through the settings menu within the Service or via email.
privacy.section7Content: The Service uses cookies to maintain login sessions. Users may refuse to store cookies through browser settings; however, some service features may be restricted in such cases.
privacy.section8Intro: The following Privacy Officer has been designated to oversee personal information processing and handle complaints and remedies related to personal information:
privacy.section9Content: This Privacy Policy may be amended due to changes in laws, policies, or services. Any changes will be announced through in-service notices.
privacy.supplementContent: This Privacy Policy shall take effect from December 1, 2024.

copyrightPage.overviewContent: WORSHEEP (hereinafter "the Service") respects copyright and complies with copyright law and related regulations. This policy provides information regarding the protection of copyrights for sheet music content within the Service.
copyrightPage.classificationIntro: Sheet music within the Service is classified as follows:
copyrightPage.officialContent: Sheet music provided through formal agreements with copyright holders or rights owners. The Service has secured lawful rights for the use of such sheet music.
copyrightPage.ugcContent: Sheet music directly uploaded by Users. Responsibility for the copyright legality of such sheet music lies with the uploading User. As an online service provider, the Service removes content upon receiving copyright infringement reports.
copyrightPage.publicDomainContent: Sheet music whose copyright protection period has expired. Under Korean copyright law, this applies to works for which 70 years have passed since the death of the author.
copyrightPage.infringementIntro: If you believe your copyrighted work is being used without authorization within the Service, please file a report following the procedure below.
copyrightPage.infringementInfoTitle: Information Required for Filing a Report
copyrightPage.infringementInfo2Value: Documentation proving copyright ownership or power of attorney
copyrightPage.infringementInfo3Value: Title, URL, or other identifying information of the sheet music
copyrightPage.infringementInfo4Value: Original song title, composer/lyricist, source, etc.
copyrightPage.infringementInfo5Value: Removal request or other remedial action
copyrightPage.procedureStep1Desc: Copyright infringement reports are received via email.
copyrightPage.procedureStep2Desc: The validity of the report is reviewed.
copyrightPage.procedureStep3Desc: If infringement is confirmed, the content is removed and the uploader is notified.
copyrightPage.procedureStep4Desc: The reporter is notified of the processing result via email.
copyrightPage.repeatOffenderIntro: The Service takes the following actions against Users who repeatedly infringe copyrights:
copyrightPage.repeatOffender1stAction: Warning and removal of the infringing content
copyrightPage.counterNoticeContent: If your content has been removed due to a copyright infringement report but you believe you have legitimate rights, you may file a counter-notice at the same email address. Please include documentation proving your lawful rights to the content when filing a counter-notice.
copyrightPage.partnershipContent: We welcome partnership inquiries from worship teams, music publishers, and individual composers/lyricists. Through partnerships, your sheet music can be provided as official content with revenue sharing based on usage.
